content.text:
<user_query>
Explore repository for identity enrichment/verification logic and data structures involving wowSummary._full, extracted_data.abn, identity_signals.abn_candidates, ABN GUD API usage, Perplexity outputs, HTML extraction, social handle extraction, and report generation/persistence (deep_cmo_reports, PDF, quota). Return exact file paths, key functions/types, and current merge precedence rules if present. Identify insertion points for: (a) hard ABN validation step between website scan and identity verification, (b) multi-source social extraction with precedence Perplexity > HTML > search parsing. Do not modify files.
</user_query>
Searching the repository for identity enrichment, ABN handling, Perplexity, HTML/social extraction, and report persistence.

Here is a concise map of what exists in this worktree and how precedence works today, plus where you would hook the two features.

---

## `wowSummary._full` and footprint UI

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\frontend\src\hooks\useCalibrationState.js` | Builds `fullExtraction` (spread of `extracted_data` + `_sources`, `_deep_sources`, `_website`, `_generated_at`) and sets `wowSummary._full` in `handleAuditSubmit` (~413–430). `handleConfirmWow` reads `wowToSave._full` to build `cmoBundle` and `PUT /business-profile` (~676–714). |
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\frontend\src\components\calibration\ChiefMarketingSummary.js` | Consumes `const full = (wowSummary?._full) || wowSummary || {}` (~113–114) for CMO fields. |
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\frontend\src\components\calibration\WowSummary.js` | Renders editable `wowSummary` fields (not `_full` directly). |

---

## `extracted_data.abn` and `identity_signals.abn_candidates`

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\supabase\functions\calibration-business-dna\index.ts` | `extractIdentitySignals()` sets `signals.abn_candidates` from regex on combined content (~184–187). After GPT JSON → `extracted`, **STEP 3** fills `extracted.abn` from `identitySignals.abn_candidates[0]` when AI is empty or placeholder (~520–523). Response returns `extracted_data: extracted`, `identity_signals: identitySignals` (~590–596). |
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\frontend\src\hooks\useCalibrationState.js` | Merges `deepEnrichment` over edge `exRaw` (many fields use `deepEnrichment.X \|\| exRaw.X`, so backend enrichment wins when set) (~382–411). `abn` line: `deepEnrichment.abn \|\| exRaw.abn` (~396). Then `parseIdentitySignals(ex, url)`, then edge `abn_candidates` / deep `abn` / deep `abn_candidates` (~439–453). |
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\frontend\src\components\calibration\ForensicIdentityCard.js` | `parseIdentitySignals()` — ABN from regex over all string fields of `extractedData`, else `d.abn` (~69–71). |

---

## ABR “GUD” / GUID API (Australian Business Register)

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\supabase\functions\business-identity-lookup\index.ts` | `ABR_GUID` with fallbacks `ABR_GUD`, `ABR_API_GUID` (~26–29). `lookupByAbn` → `https://abr.business.gov.au/json/AbnDetails.aspx?...&guid=` (~52–54). `searchByName` → `MatchingNames.aspx` (~70–71). Used from the UI via `handleAbnLookup` → `callEdgeFunction('business-identity-lookup', ...)` — **manual** lookup on the identity card, not an automatic pipeline gate. |

---

## Perplexity vs HTML vs Firecrawl (edge scan)

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\supabase\functions\calibration-business-dna\index.ts` | `deepSearch()` → Perplexity `sonar-pro` (~150–168). Five parallel queries for identity/services/market/team/competitors (~374–404). Firecrawl: `scrapeWebsiteWithKeyPages` / `scrapeWebsite` (~412–429). If no crawl content: `fetchWebsiteHtml` + `stripHtmlTags` (~432–443). `allContent = [perplexityContent, websiteContent, businessDescription].join` (~469–470) feeds **deterministic** `extractIdentitySignals`. GPT `contextBlock` labels Perplexity as **PRIMARY** and website as **SUPPLEMENTAL** (~476–479). `EXTRACTION_PROMPT` states conflict rule: official site > government registry > news (~299–301). |

---

## Backend “search parsing” enrichment (Trinity / Serper)

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\backend\routes\calibration.py` | `website_enrichment` `POST /calibration/enrichment/website` — `scrape_url_text`, raw HTML fetch, Serper searches (`company_search`, `competitor_search`, `abn_search`), `combined_text` (~907–912), `_extract_abn_candidates(combined_text)` (~913), `_extract_social_handles_from_html(raw_html)` (~952), then AI synthesis merge (~971–974), then regex over `combined_text` for any social platform still empty (~986–997). |

---

## Social extraction (current behaviour)

| Source | Path / behaviour |
|--------|------------------|
| Edge regex | `calibration-business-dna` `extractIdentitySignals` — `socialPatterns` per platform, **first** URL match in `allContent` (~203–217). Because `allContent` is **Perplexity then website**, first hit per platform tends to favour earlier text (Perplexity). |
| GPT | `extracted.social_media_links` merged in STEP 3 only where deterministic has a URL and extracted platform slot is empty (~536–540). |
| Frontend | `parseIdentitySignals` — regex over concatenated string `values` of `extractedData`, then adds `d.social_media_links` entries not already present (~74–89). Order of `Object.values` is not a stable “precedence” contract. |
| Backend enrichment | `_extract_social_handles_from_html` then search-result `combined_text` regex (~952, ~986–997). |

---

## Report persistence / PDF / quota

| Topic | Path / note |
|-------|-------------|
| **Calibration persistence** | `useCalibrationState.js` `autoSave` → `POST /console/state` (~213–215); `COMPLETE` also upserts `strategic_console_state` and `user_operator_profile` (~221–234). |
| **CMO footprint → profile** | `handleConfirmWow` → `PUT /business-profile` with `competitor_scan_result: JSON.stringify(cmoBundle)` (~691–714). |
| **Generic PDF** | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\backend\routes\reports.py` — `generate_pdf` / `serve_pdf`. |
| **`deep_cmo_reports` table** | Described in `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\docs\CALIBRATION_2_PRODUCT_SPEC_V1.md` (~230–251); **no matching implementation** found in this repo (no migration/file named `deep_cmo`). |
| **Quota** | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\backend\routes\deps.py` — `check_rate_limit`, `RATE_LIMIT_FEATURE_LABELS` (soundboard, trinity, boardroom, war_room) (~111–116, ~207–251). **No `deep_cmo` feature** in this list; product-spec quotas are not wired here. |
| **Reports UI** | `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\frontend\src\pages\ReportsPage.js` — PDF export path noted as not fully available in UI. |

---

## Merge precedence (as implemented)

1. **Frontend after scan (`handleAuditSubmit`)**  
   Base = edge `extracted_data`; overlay = `deepEnrichment` from `POST /calibration/enrichment/website` where each field uses `deepEnrichment.X \|\| exRaw.X` (deep wins when truthy).  
   Identity: `parseIdentitySignals(ex)` then fill gaps from `edgeSignals.abn_candidates`, `deepEnrichment.abn`, `deepEnrichment.abn_candidates`, then contact/geo/social from edge only if empty; then `ex.abn` / `ex.address` if still missing (~439–486).

2. **Edge `calibration-business-dna`**  
   - Regex pool: `perplexityContent` **before** `websiteContent` in `allContent`.  
   - GPT: prompt authority = official site > registry > news.  
   - **STEP 3 “deterministic wins”**: fills `extracted.abn`, `business_name`, `contact_*`, `address`, `social_media_links` when AI is null/placeholder (~520–540).

3. **Backend `/enrichment/website`**  
   Deterministic `abn`/`abn_candidates` from regex on `combined_text`; `social_handles` from HTML then `combined_text`; AI `parsed` **updates** `enrichment` keys where present (~971–974).

---

## Insertion points (design only)

### (a) Hard ABN validation between website scan and identity verification

Natural seams:

1. **Frontend (minimal change to UX flow)** — `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\frontend\src\hooks\useCalibrationState.js` inside `handleAuditSubmit`, **after** `auditData` + optional `deepEnrichment` merge and **before** `setWowSummary` / `setIdentitySignals` / `setEntry("identity_verification")` (~437–494). You could call `business-identity-lookup` (or a new backend endpoint that wraps ABR) with the candidate ABN and gate or flag invalid numbers.

2. **Edge (server-side truth before UI)** — `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\supabase\functions\calibration-business-dna\index.ts` **after** STEP 3 merge (~520+) and **before** `return` (~590+), optionally calling ABR JSON APIs (same as `business-identity-lookup`) to validate `extracted.abn` / `abn_candidates[0]` and annotate `identity_signals` or strip invalid ABNs.

3. **Backend enrichment** — `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup\backend\routes\calibration.py` in `website_enrichment` after `abn_candidates` are built (~913–951), before returning the draft payload.

Current flow already separates **scan** from **identity step** via `setEntry("identity_verification")` after scan; validation fits between merged data and that transition.

### (b) Multi-source social extraction with precedence Perplexity > HTML > search parsing

Current code does **not** implement that explicit precedence end-to-end:

- **Edge**: `allContent` order already puts Perplexity before HTML for regex; GPT merge fills only empty slots from deterministic (~536–540). To enforce strict precedence explicitly, adjust **`extractIdentitySignals`** (or split three buffers and merge in order) in `calibration-business-dna\index.ts` (~171–248, ~469–471).

- **Backend**: `combined_text` interleaves page text and Serper snippets; HTML extraction runs first, then search regex (~952, ~986–997). To match **Perplexity > HTML > search**, you would need a Perplexity (or edge) social pass first, then `_extract_social_handles_from_html`, then Serper `combined_text` — **`website_enrichment` in `calibration.py`** is the right place.

- **Frontend**: **`parseIdentitySignals`** in `ForensicIdentityCard.js` (~59–114) merges regex + `social_media_links` without a clear ordering; **`handleAuditSubmit`** edge social merge (~466–469) only runs when `signals.socials` is empty. For unified precedence, implement a small helper that takes `{ perplexity, html, search }` and merge in order, then call it from `useCalibrationState.js` after `ex` is assembled (or pass structured sources from the edge response).

---

## Key types / functions (quick index)

| Location | Symbols |
|----------|---------|
| `supabase/functions/calibration-business-dna/index.ts` | `extractIdentitySignals`, `deepSearch`, `scrapeWebsite`, `fetchWebsiteHtml`, `EXTRACTION_PROMPT`, serve handler STEPS 1–4 |
| `supabase/functions/business-identity-lookup/index.ts` | `lookupByAbn`, `searchByName`, `scoreMatch` |
| `backend/routes/calibration.py` | `_extract_abn_candidates`, `_extract_social_handles_from_html`, `website_enrichment` |
| `frontend/.../ForensicIdentityCard.js` | `parseIdentitySignals`, `computeIdentityConfidence` |
| `frontend/.../useCalibrationState.js` | `handleAuditSubmit`, `handleConfirmWow`, `handleAbnLookup`, `autoSave` |

No files were modified; this is read-only exploration of `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\oup`.
